questions.question: Which two ocean sustainability programs certify Caruso's seafood sourcing?
questions.explanation: Smart Catch (Monterey Bay Aquarium program) and Ocean Wise (Canadian conservation org) both evaluate species health and catch practices. If a species is flagged, it leaves the menu.

questions.question: What is unique about the honey used in Caruso's dishes and cocktails?
options: It comes from a beehive on the hotel property
It is a blend of three California wildflowers
It is purchased from a Montecito farmers market
questions.explanation: The honey comes from a resident beehive maintained on the Miramar Beach property — the bees forage on coastal wildflowers, giving the honey a floral, lightly salted character.

questions.question: Caruso's holds a Michelin Green Star in addition to its Michelin Star. What does the Green Star specifically recognize?
questions.explanation: The Michelin Green Star, introduced in 2020, specifically recognizes restaurants leading on sustainability — separate from culinary excellence. Caruso's is one of very few US recipients of both.

questions.question: What percentage of Caruso's ingredients are locally sourced within California?
options: The menu varies — no fixed target
questions.explanation: Nearly 100% of Caruso's ingredients are locally sourced — this is Chef Falsini's non-negotiable standard. If it can't be grown, raised, or caught in California, it doesn't belong on the menu.

questions.question: Chef Massimo Falsini began his culinary career as:
options: A line cook in New York
questions.explanation: Chef Falsini started as a dishwasher in Rome and worked his way through Michelin-starred kitchens across Europe before arriving in Montecito. The story connects Italian soul to California terroir.

questions.question: What does the name 'Massaman' most likely derive from?
options: The name of a spice village in Southern Thailand
A chef who invented the dish in the 19th century
questions.explanation: Massaman curry entered Thailand via Muslim spice trade routes from the Middle East and Persia. The dish name is believed to derive from 'Musulman' — reflecting its cultural origins.

questions.question: How long has Chef Bua (Pattama Chocklapphon) been practising royal Thai cuisine?
questions.explanation: Chef Bua has been practising royal Thai culinary traditions for over 30 years — she is not a contemporary chef exploring the tradition, she is a keeper of it.

questions.question: Which farm supplies Lakorn's free-range chicken?
questions.explanation: Klongpai Farm supplies welfare-certified free-range chicken — no growth hormones, outdoor access required. Sampran Farm is the pork supplier; Khunta is beef; Raitong is the organic rice farm.

questions.question: What transformation happens to durian when it is fried for the Massaman dish?
options: Its pungency disappears and it becomes nutty and caramelized
Its texture becomes similar to tofu
It absorbs the curry's flavor completely
questions.explanation: Deep-frying durian removes its notorious pungency and creates a rich, caramelized, nutty crust — a completely different eating experience. It is considered the 'King of Fruits' in Thai culture.

questions.question: Lakorn's menu covers how many regional Thai culinary traditions?
options: Four — Northern, Northeastern, Southern, and Central
Three — Bangkok, Chiang Mai, and Phuket styles
One — it focuses on royal Bangkok cuisine only
questions.explanation: Lakorn's 2024 repositioning was built around showcasing all four regions of Thailand: Northern, Northeastern (Isaan), Southern, and Central. Chef Bua and Executive Chef Narongrit together cover all four.

questions.question: Who supplies all the chicken at Pirules — and what makes this supplier unique?
options: Traspatio — a women-led cooperative whose profits go directly to rural women members
Vía Orgánica — a regenerative ranch in the Jalpan Valley
La Factoría — a local dairy farm with free-range poultry
Comepesca — a sustainable fishing cooperative that also raises poultry
questions.explanation: Traspatio is a women-led cooperative — every chicken order at Pirules directly funds the livelihoods of rural women in the Bajío region. No middlemen. This is the most powerful table story at this property.

questions.question: What percentage of Pirules' dishes are cooked over open fire — ash, wood, or charcoal?
questions.explanation: 80% of all dishes at Pirules are cooked using fire techniques — ash roasting, wood smoke, or charcoal searing. This is an ancestral Central Mexican practice, not a trend.

questions.question: What is the sourcing radius Chef Odín Rocha operates under at Pirules?
options: 200 km — all of the Bajío region
Within San Miguel de Allende city limits
questions.explanation: Chef Rocha's 60-mile rule requires that 99% of every ingredient come from within 60 miles of the restaurant. The 1% exception is essentially salt. This constraint shapes every single dish on the menu.

questions.question: What makes La Factoría's dairy products medically distinctive?
options: They use genetic analysis to breed casein-free cattle — accessible to guests with dairy sensitivities
Their milk is certified A2 and ultra-pasteurized
They are the only certified organic dairy within 100 miles
Their cattle are fed on maguey leaves and nopal cactus
questions.explanation: La Factoría uses genetic analysis to identify and breed casein-free cattle. This produces milk, butter, and cheese that is naturally accessible to guests with common dairy sensitivities — without any industrial substitutes.

questions.question: What technique is used for Pirules' barbacoa, and how old is it?
options: Overnight pit roast lined with maguey leaves — a pre-Hispanic technique over 2,000 years old
Slow charcoal smoking over 12 hours — a colonial-era adaptation
Wood-fired oven roasting at low temperature — 19th century method
Open-flame spit roasting — indigenous technique from Oaxaca
questions.explanation: The barbacoa is slow-roasted overnight in a pit lined with maguey (agave) leaves — a pre-Hispanic preservation technique unchanged in 2,000 years. The leaves seal moisture and add a subtle mineral character.
